does: This paper presents DeepPersona, a generative system for creating highly detailed synthetic personas by instilling them into large language models. The core contribution involves a two-stage approach: first, mining thousands of real ChatGPT conversations to construct a large hierarchical taxonomy of human attributes organized into hundreds of categories; second, progressively sampling from this taxonomy to conditionally generate coherent personas that encode roughly 1 MB of narrative text and hundreds of structured attributes. The method produces personas that are two orders of magnitude more detailed and complex than prior synthetic persona systems, with intrinsic evaluations demonstrating 32 percent higher attribute diversity and improved attribute preservation compared to existing approaches.
connects: This work builds on the growing literature of synthetic persona generation for LLM-based agent simulation, extending prior research that demonstrated the feasibility of instilling personas into language models for behavioral modeling and alignment research. It responds to documented limitations of existing methods, which create shallow personas with minimal attribute coverage that fail to capture human complexity. The taxonomy construction approach leverages recent advances in mining knowledge from large-scale conversational data, connecting to work on extracting structured human knowledge from language model interactions. The research also relates to personalization frameworks in NLP and ongoing efforts to improve human-AI alignment through more nuanced behavioral simulation.
enables: DeepPersona enables more realistic behavioral simulations for agent-based research by providing a scalable foundation for creating personas with rich, multifaceted identities. This capability could support advances in studying alignment between synthetic and real human behavior, developing more personalized and context-aware AI systems, and conducting behavioral experiments at previously unattainable levels of granularity. The taxonomy itself represents a reusable resource for attribute-driven persona synthesis across multiple downstream applications. The approach points toward future work on validating whether increased persona depth translates to improved alignment metrics, exploring how narrative-rich personas influence downstream model behavior in multi-agent simulations, and extending the method to construct specialized persona taxonomies for domain-specific research.
